content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of size NNN and an integer KKK. He can perform the following operation on AAA any number of times:


Select any KKK distinct indices i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iKi_1, i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äã and increment the array elements at these KKK indices by 111.

Formally, set Aij:=Aij+1A_{i_j} := A_{i_j} + 1Aij‚Äã‚Äã:=Aij‚Äã‚Äã+1 for all 1‚â§j‚â§K1 \le j \le K1‚â§j‚â§K.
For example, if A=[3,2,8,4,6]A = [3, 2, 8, 4, 6]A=[3,2,8,4,6] and we select the indices 2,3,52, 3, 52,3,5, then AAA becomes [3,2+1,8+1,4,6+1][3, 2 + 1, 8 + 1, 4, 6 + 1][3,2+1,8+1,4,6+1] i.e. [3,3,9,4,7][3, 3, 9, 4, 7][3,3,9,4,7].

Determine if Chef can make the array AAA palindromic by applying the given operation **any** number of times.

Note: An array is called palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [4,10,10,4][4, 10, 10, 4][4,10,10,4] and [7,1,7][7, 1, 7][7,1,7] are palindromic arrays.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the size of the array AAA and the parameter mentioned in the statement.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if we can make AAA palindromic by applying the given operation. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given array AAA is already palindromic.
Test case 222: We can apply the following operations:

Select index [4][4][4]: AAA becomes [4,5,5,5,6,4][4, 5, 5, 5, 6, 4][4,5,5,5,6,4]
Select index [2][2][2]: AAA becomes [4,6,5,5,6,4][4, 6, 5, 5, 6, 4][4,6,5,5,6,4]

Test case 333: We can apply the following operations:

Select index [2,4][2, 4][2,4]: AAA becomes [4,6,5,5,6,4][4, 6, 5, 5, 6, 4][4,6,5,5,6,4]

Test case 444: It can be proven that AAA can not be converted into a palindrome using the given operations.
